library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is mostly standalone, but one exported public class, com.sun.istack.tools.ProtectedTask, directly extends and uses Apache Ant APIs while org.apache.ant:ant is declared optional in the POM, so normal transitive resolution will not add it and meaningful coverage of that class needs an explicit test dependency.

deterministic_setup.reason: istack-commons-tools 4.1.2 declares org.apache.ant:ant as an optional dependency, but its public ProtectedTask API extends org.apache.tools.ant.Task and implements DynamicConfigurator; tests that subclass or execute ProtectedTask need these Ant classes on the test classpath.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or external service is indicated. For DefaultAuthenticator coverage, create temporary auth files and set/clear http.proxyUser/http.proxyPassword or proxyUser/proxyPassword inside the test if exercising proxy branches, restoring global Authenticator state with reset/cleanup. For ProtectedTask coverage, use a minimal in-test subclass and dummy task/classloader setup rather than invoking external Ant builds.
risks: ProtectedTask manipulates the thread context ClassLoader and closes Closeable classloaders, so tests should isolate and restore global/thread state carefully.
DefaultAuthenticator installs a JVM-global default Authenticator; missing cleanup can make tests order-dependent.
Native Image verification remains authoritative for any dynamic classloading or Authenticator behavior.
